heading: Report on environmental problems in Poland and Hungary
label: 5473 Report on environmental problems in Poland and Hungary

children.label: A
children.text: a relative ranking of the severity of the problems and their effects on both human health and the general environment;

children.label: B
children.text: a listing of the geographical areas of each country that have suffered the heaviest environmental damage, and a description of the source and scope of the damage; and

children.label: C
children.text: an assessment of the environmental performance of leading industrial polluters in those countries and the expected effect on pollution levels of industrial modernization.

children.heading: Priorities and costs for action
children.label: 2 Priorities and costs for action
children.text: An analysis of the priorities that Poland and Hungary should each assign in addressing its environmental problems, and an estimate of the capital and human resources required to undertake a comprehensive program of environmental protection in that country.

children.heading: Role of United States and multilateral assistance
children.label: 3 Role of United States and multilateral assistance
A
children.text: recommendations for appropriate levels and forms of bilateral financial and technical assistance;

children.label: B
children.text: recommendations concerning United States participation in cooperative multilateral undertakings;

children.label: C
children.text: an assessment of the feasibility of debt-for-nature swaps as a technique of environmental protection in each country; and

children.label: D
children.text: recommendations for minimizing further environmental damage to Krakow, and for the protection and restoration of historic sites in that city.
